sections.paragraph:
εὐδία, ας, ἡ (εὔδιος ‘calm, clear’ [the early gen. of Ζεύς is Διός])

① fair weather (X., Pla. et al.; Plut., Mor. 8c; POxy 1223, 12; Sir 3:15; Philo, Gig. 51) Mt 16:2.

② oft. in imagery, to represent peace and rest (opp. χειμών: Pind., al.; X., An. 5, 8, 20; Epict. 2, 18, 30), then in the mng. tranquillity, peace (Herodas 1, 28; OGI 90, 11 [196 b.c.] τ. Αἴγυπτον εἰς εὐδίαν ἀγαγεῖν; Philo, Spec. Leg. 1, 69; Jos., Ant. 14, 157) ISm 11:3.—DELG. M-M. Spicq.

gen. gen. = genitive

  1. X. = Xenophon, V–IV b.c.—List 5

Pla Pla , V–IV b.c.; s. also Ps.-Plato—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

Plut Plut , I–II a.d.—List 5

POxy POxy = Oxyrhynchus Papyri—List 4

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

oft. oft. = often

opp. opp. = opposed to, opposite

Pind Pind , V b.c.—List 5

  1. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)

Epict Epict , various works, I–II a.d.—List 5

mng. mng. = meaning(s)

Herodas Herodas , a.k.a. Herondas, III b.c.—List 5

OGI OGI = Orientis Graeci Inscriptiones Selectae—List 3

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

ISm ISm = Ignatius to the Smyrnaeans, I–II a.d.—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Spicq Spicq = CS., Lexique théologique du Nouveau Testament—Lists 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 404
